notes: kiro-cli is the FORK variant of checkpoint-rewind (the kiro analogue of codex /fork). Source is FilesUnderRoot(~/.kiro/sessions/cli): a launch pre-session enters ready and hands off to a UUID-1 session on the first prompt. UUID-1 runs TWO normal turns ('one','two') — the checkpoints to rewind back from. Then /rewind opens an arrow-key picker; selecting an earlier prompt forks into a NEW session (UUID-2, a fresh <uuid>.jsonl under the watched root) that replays the pre-rewind history; the original UUID-1 file lingers (kiro emits NO transcript_removed at rewind time, the row stays ready until process exit). The post-rewind turn ('three') runs on UUID-2 from the rewound point. Three user-observable signals: (1) the session stays ready after the rewind (the fork is not a turn), asserted as a post_rewind_ready dwell; (2) a NEW session_id appears (kiro forks rather than rewinding in place like claudecode) — asserted as v2_session_birth new_session:true, the strongest observable that the conversation was rewound into a fresh session; (3) the post-rewind turn drives a clean ready→working→ready arc on UUID-2. agent_supports=partial: /rewind reverts conversation state only (continues in a new session from an earlier prompt; no file-edit checkpoint restore). Provenance caveat (not asserted, not a bug): the kiro JSONL has no forked-from field and nothing sets ParentSessionID, so the daemon surfaces the fork as an independent new session_id rather than a parent-linked child — the new row + arc are still observed, only the linkage label is lost (same shape as codex/checkpoint-rewind). DRIVER GAP (keys): the recipe drives the /rewind picker with `keys` (Down/Enter); the kiro driver does not yet elicit `keys` (route driver-gap) — record ports the keys arm from drive-claudecode-interactive.sh before driving. Observations: kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost, so model unknown, cost_nonzero false, tokens_nonzero false.

text: Launch pre-session row appears in ready within 2s of kiro-cli chat launch
PID bound by cwd match within 2s

After the first prompt lands, kiro writes its <uuid>.jsonl and the keyed UUID-1 session appears in ready — the pre-session hands off to a fresh UUID-1 (bound generous to cover first-token latency before kiro writes its transcript)
First user prompt ('one') drives ready → working on the UUID-1 session
First turn completes back to ready (text-only AssistantMessage) on UUID-1 — checkpoint 1
Second user prompt ('two') drives ready → working on the same UUID-1
Second turn completes back to ready on UUID-1 — checkpoint 2, the point to rewind back from

Spec: 'Session row reflects the rewound state with state ready' — after the /rewind picker confirm, the session stays in ready (the fork itself is not a turn) and the original UUID-1 file lingers (no transcript_removed).

Spec: 'a linked new session_id if the adapter forks' — /rewind forks into a fresh UUID-2 (<uuid>.jsonl) never seen before, restored from the rewound point; this is the strongest observable that kiro forked the conversation into a new session.
Spec: 'Subsequent turns start cleanly from the rewound point: ready → working' — the post-rewind prompt ('three') drives the NEW session ready → working (must NOT match a stale transition on UUID-1).
Spec: post-rewind turn completes back to ready on the new session UUID-2 — a clean ready → working → ready arc from the rewound point; final live state is ready.
